title: two_real_notes_shared_anchor uses shared commitment tree — the only correct multi-input approach
description: Happy-path tests with 2 input notes use two_real_notes_shared_anchor(), which places both note CMUs in the same commitment tree before creating witnesses. Witnesses from independent trees have different roots → AnchorMismatch before reaching the prover. More subtly: witnesses built with arbitrary leaf bytes (as in make_witness for error tests) are not valid for any real note CMU — using them in happy-path tests would fail at proof generation ('note commitment not in tree'). The shared-tree approach is the only option that produces witnesses valid for their respective notes AND sharing the same anchor. A contributor who tries to simplify by reusing make_witness for happy-path tests will get confusing prover failures, not a clean test.
close_reason: decision records documented in beads; no code change required
